entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Cereal crop grown in semi-arid tropics for food, feed, fodder, and fuel value

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: Geographic region characterized by semi-arid conditions where sorghum is grown

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Continent and center of origin for sorghum

entities.name: BIOTIC AND ABIOTIC STRESSES
entities.description: Environmental and biological factors that constrain sorghum production

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced per unit area, a target for genetic enhancement

entities.name: STOVER YIELD
entities.description: The amount of vegetative biomass produced, a target for genetic enhancement

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL QUALITY
entities.description: The nutrient composition of the crop, a target for genetic enhancement

entities.name: PLANT DEFENSE TRAITS
entities.description: Characteristics that protect the plant from biotic and abiotic stresses to stabilize performance

entities.name: CROP BOTANY
entities.description: The study of the physical and biological structure of the crop

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of the sorghum species

entities.name: GENETICS
entities.description: The study of genes, genetic variation, and heredity in sorghum

entities.name: CROP-BREEDING STRATEGIES
entities.description: Planned approaches to improve the genetic makeup of the crop

entities.name: FLORAL BIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of the structure and function of flowers in sorghum

entities.name: CLASSIFICATION
entities.description: The systematic arrangement of sorghum into groups based on characteristics

entities.name: BREEDING METHODS
entities.description: Specific techniques used to achieve genetic improvement in crops

entities.name: TAXONOMY
entities.description: The science of naming, describing, and classifying organisms
